raw_consumer_statement:
I am writing to formally dispute the delivery status of a blazer I purchased from XXXX for approximately {$2700.00}, which was scheduled to be delivered on XX/XX/XXXX. 

I never received this package. 

The proof of delivery XXXX is relying on is a single photograph provided by the shipping XXXX showing a box placed in a generic hallway. The image does not show : - A door number - An apartment number - A visible address - Any identifiable feature tying the location to my residence There is no way to verify that the photo was taken at my building, let alone at my unit. As such, this does not constitute valid proof of delivery. A photo of a box in an unmarked hallway could have been taken in virtually any building in XXXX. 

Despite this, XXXX is presenting this image as conclusive evidence of delivery, and American Express is relying on Nordstroms assertion in reviewing my dispute. This places me in an unfair position, as I am being held responsible for a carriers failure to properly document delivery and a merchants acceptance of inadequate proof. 

To be clear : - I did not receive the merchandise- There was no signature confirmation - There is no verifiable delivery evidence tied to my address I am requesting that XXXX : XXXX. Acknowledge that the provided image does not meet reasonable delivery verification standards, and XXXX. Either issue a refund or ship a replacement immediately If this matter can not be resolved promptly, I will continue escalating through my card issuer and applicable consumer protection channels, as I have acted in good faith at every step. 

Please confirm next steps in writing. 

XXXX XXXX
